author: Reilly Wood
message:
Hide masked and not-found units by default

not-found units are dangling references that can't be started, and masked
units are deliberately disabled; together they were 10-19% of the default
list on machines I checked. The services panel title now shows how many
units the status filter is hiding, e.g. "Services (44 hidden)".

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Fix flaky click-to-select check in remote mode

The remote (ssh localhost) integration run failed on master: the click
selected the right unit, but the services list was still loading and
re-sorted before the assertion, moving the selected unit to a different
row (before=4 target=5 after=9). Compare the unit name under the
highlight instead of the row number.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Compare only the logs pane in the wheel-scroll test

The whole-screen comparison was unstable: the details pane shows
relative timestamps like '(17s ago)' that tick between captures, which
made it look like End had scrolled on the slower container runs.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Fix flaky mouse tests in CI

Two problems, both in the tests rather than the app:

1. The 'select systemd-journald' step took the first fuzzy-search match,
   which on several distros was the systemd-journald@ template unit
   (empty description, no logs), breaking every check after it. Now it
   clicks the exact-match row and waits for real journald log lines to
   appear before continuing.
2. The wheel-scroll check asserted content changed, but on freshly
   booted CI runners journald's logs fit entirely in the pane, so the
   clamped scroll offset correctly stays at 0. When the wheel does
   nothing, the test now verifies End can't scroll either.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Fix TUI corruption from native clipboard writes

Copying via mouse could print an arboard warning over the TUI:
'Clipboard was dropped very quickly after writing...'. The warning was
right - on X11/Wayland the writing process must stay alive to serve the
paste, so clipboard_anywhere's write-and-drop call could never work
reliably (a latent bug in the existing 'Copy unit file path' action
too, which shared it).

Copies now always emit OSC 52, plus best-effort pipe the text to a
system clipboard utility (wl-copy/xclip/xsel/pbcopy) with output
nulled. Those tools fork and keep serving the selection themselves.
This drops the clipboard-anywhere dependency, and the toast now just
says 'Copied N chars' since OSC 52 isn't necessarily the only path.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Add mouse interaction coverage to the integration tests

Injects raw SGR mouse escape sequences through tmux send-keys to test:
click-to-select in the services list, click-to-copy details fields (via
the toast), wheel scrolling and drag-selection in the logs pane, and
click behavior of the action menu and status filter popups. Rows are
located by scanning captured output rather than hardcoding coordinates.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Complete mouse support: action/signal menus, help and error popups

Every interactive surface now handles the mouse: menu popups get
hover-to-highlight, click-to-execute, wheel navigation, and
click-outside-to-close; the help and error popups dismiss on click.
Only the Processing overlay still swallows mouse input (intentionally).

Also unified the two clipboard paths: the 'Copy unit file path' menu
action used clipboard_anywhere (native, fails over SSH) while mouse
copies used OSC 52 (works over SSH, unsupported in some old terminals).
All copies now do both, and all show the toast.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Make the status filter popup mouse-interactive

The status filter arrived on master while the mouse work was in flight,
so it had no mouse support at all - worse, clicks leaked through the
popup to the service list underneath. Now: click a filter to toggle it,
hover moves the cursor, wheel scrolls through entries, and clicking
outside the popup closes it.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Polish mouse copy: all details fields clickable, better toast

- Every details field (description, enablement, scope, load/active
  state, unit file path) is now hover-highlighted and click-to-copy,
  not just the path
- Hover is bold only (no underline)
- Toast says how many chars were copied and that it went via OSC 52
- Toast moved to the very bottom row, flush right, and drawn last so
  the help line no longer paints over it

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Make services list and search bar clickable

Clicking a service selects it (and hands focus back to the list when
searching), clicking the search bar focuses search, and mouse input is
ignored while modal dialogs are open. Also swapped our hand-rolled
rect-contains helper and inner-rect math for ratatui's built-in
Rect::contains and Rect::inner.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Add hover+click-to-copy for unit file path, and a copy toast

The unit file path in the details pane now goes bold+underlined on
mouseover, and a single click copies the full path (from the unit data,
not the possibly-truncated screen text). Both copy paths - this and log
text selection - now show a transient 'Copied to clipboard' toast in
the bottom right for 2 seconds.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Add mouse support: text selection + copy in logs pane, wheel scrolling

Drag in the logs pane to select text (highlighted with reverse video)
and copy it to the clipboard on release via OSC 52, which works both
locally and over SSH. The wheel scrolls the logs, or moves the service
selection when over the list.

Selection reads text straight out of the render buffer rather than
mapping screen cells back through paragraph wrapping, so it only covers
visible text - same as native terminal selection, which mouse capture
otherwise takes away.

author: Reilly Wood
message:
Fix scroll-to-bottom in logs pane using Paragraph::line_count

The wrapped height of the logs paragraph is now computed at render time
via ratatui's unstable-rendered-line-info feature, and the scroll offset
is clamped to it. This fixes End scrolling past the logs into blank
space, and PageDown/ctrl+d overshooting the end.
